input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** ‚Äî use `AppThemeBinding` to declare light/dark values inline:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Spacing="16" Padding="20">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device theme at startup **and** reacts to live changes ‚Äî no event wiring needed.

---

**C# equivalent** ‚Äî use `SetAppTheme<T>()` or `SetAppThemeColor()`:

```csharp
// For Color properties (shorthand)
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Colors.Black,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

// For any BindableProperty (generic version)
page.SetAppTheme<Color>(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Colors.White,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

button.SetAppTheme<Color>(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

---

**Key points:**

| | XAML | C# |
|---|---|---|
| Syntax | `{AppThemeBinding Light=‚Ä¶, Dark=‚Ä¶}` | `.SetAppThemeColor()` / `.SetAppTheme<T>()` |
| Live updates | ‚úÖ automatic | ‚úÖ automatic |
| Works with | any bindable property | any bindable property |

- Both respond to `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` changing at runtime.
- You can also detect the current theme manually via `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` (returns `AppTheme.Light`, `Dark`, or `Unspecified`).
- For larger apps, define themed colors in `ResourceDictionary` and reference them with `{StaticResource}` or `{DynamicResource}` for cleaner maintenance.
